mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly reducing present equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean when FEDFUNDS increases, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 5.0 percent, labor market slack signals deteriorating aggregate demand and rising recession probability, which forces equity risk premiums higher and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in greater than 55 percent of instances where UNRATE crosses above 5.0, or out-of-sample Sharpe ratio falls below 0.1.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of rising retail sales and declining federal funds rates indicates strong consumer demand supported by cheap credit, which accelerates corporate revenue growth and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when RSAFS growth exceeds 2 percent and FEDFUNDS falls, or the interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant at p greater than 0.10 in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding industrial production reflects robust manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which translates into higher near-term corporate profit margins and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to precede positive 5-day returns during recovery phases, or the signal reverses direction when capacity utilization exceeds 85 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real gross domestic product growth directly increases the denominator in valuation multiples and boosts aggregate corporate earnings, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDPC1 growth shows no statistical correlation with 60-day forward returns, or Rank IC remains below 0.01 across all rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index crosses above 260.0, accelerating inflation erodes real consumer purchasing power and forces central banks to tighten policy, which compresses equity multiples and yields negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL readings above 260.0 coincide with positive 20-day forward returns in greater than 60 percent of cases, or the threshold effect disappears after controlling for nominal interest rates.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in total nonfarm payrolls and housing starts indicates synchronized labor market strength and construction sector expansion, which amplifies economic momentum and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint PAYEMS and HOUST growth signal produces negative forward returns when housing inventory exceeds 6 months, or the interaction effect is indistinguishable from zero in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: When the industrial production index crosses below 105.0, manufacturing contraction signals weakening economic activity and declining corporate revenue expectations, which triggers negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: INDPRO readings below 105.0 are followed by positive 20-day returns in greater than 50 percent of instances, or the predictive power vanishes when controlling for monetary policy shifts.
